text.title: code-review Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for code-review as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent suggests a fix calling a function or method that does not exist on the version of the library pinned in the project.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs have training data from many library versions. Without version-pinned context, the agent suggests APIs that existed in older or newer versions but not the one in use. The author spends time investigating a non-existent function.

failure-mode.description: Agent silently reviews only the head of a large diff and produces findings that ignore the tail of the PR, with no indication that truncation occurred.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: PR diffs larger than the agent's context window are silently truncated. The agent produces a complete-looking review that misses the last 40% of changes. Reviewers trusting the agent output approve PRs with unreviewed code.

failure-mode.description: Agent flags SQL injection on parameterized queries, CSRF on internal services, or XSS on sanitized inputs — generating false-positive security findings that erode reviewer trust.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs pattern-match on surface appearance rather than runtime behavior. A string that looks like a SQL fragment in a log message triggers injection warnings even when the actual query path is parameterized. After a few false positives, reviewers stop reading security findings.

failure-mode.description: Review output consists of 80% formatting and naming nitpicks from a mixed-formatter codebase, drowning out the BLOCKING correctness and design findings.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without a pre-agent lint pass, the agent sees formatting inconsistencies across the entire diff and produces a high-volume of style comments. These comments make the review appear thorough while hiding the findings that actually matter.

failure-mode.description: Sending a full PR diff to a third-party LLM API without scanning for secrets first, potentially exposing API keys, passwords, or tokens in the diff.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Developers occasionally commit secrets accidentally. Sending that diff to an external API creates a second exposure vector. The secret may appear in API logs, rate-limit tracking, or training data.
